_comment: Published benchmark figures used for validation. Every number carries its source. Do not tune models to match these; compare and explain.

big_one_m72_wvf.phivolcs_max_credible_source: PHIVOLCS Director Bacolcol, 2026: 'maximum credible earthquake that will be generated by the West Valley Fault is only magnitude 7.2'; Solidum 2015: ~100 km rupture -> M7.2, 'don't ever think there will be a magnitude 8 or 9 from this fault'
big_one_m72_wvf.paleoseismic_upper_bound_source: Geological/paleoseismic assessments of the Marikina Valley Fault System place the maximum estimated magnitude near 7.5; scarp morphology studies (Rimando et al.) support M>7 capability on WVF segment I. Treated here as epistemic uncertainty on Mmax.
big_one_m72_wvf.expected_building_damage_source: PHIVOLCS-Australia risk analysis study, 2013 (cited at Valley Fault System Atlas launch, 2015): ~PHP 2.4T damage to buildings, ~37,000 immediate deaths
big_one_m72_wvf.world_bank_total_loss_source: World Bank Metro Manila earthquake risk estimates (MMEIRS lineage), ~USD 48B / ~12% of GDP
big_one_m72_wvf.expected_intensity: PEIS/MMI VIII across the near-fault Metro Manila corridor (PHIVOLCS)

bohol_2013_backtest.infrastructure_damage_source: NDRRMC SitRep No. 35, 2013-11-03 (also Lagmay & Eco 2014, NHESS brief communication)
bohol_2013_backtest.monetized_caveat: The PHP 2.257B figure covers public infrastructure only. The 73,002 damaged houses were counted in units, not pesos, so total direct economic loss exceeds this floor and is poorly constrained. Treat as a LOWER BOUND on total direct loss; cross-check against the EM-DAT total-damage entry for this event once the EM-DAT export is available.
bohol_2013_backtest.observed_intensity: PEIS VIII near epicenter (Sagbayan/Catigbian corridor); PEIS VII across the rest of Bohol, Tagbilaran, and Metro Cebu (PHIVOLCS isoseismal; Perez et al.)
